sessions.title: Substrate Architecture, Hypergraphs, and Modular Design
sessions.summary: Session focused on graph formalism decisions for the Data Transformation System. Key decisions made and recorded.
decisions: Hybrid representation model (leaf attributes) rejected — counter to design philosophy. Complexity absorbed upward through hierarchy.
Base substrate: directed graphs, binary edge types (structural/operational), no node typing, no weights in prototype.

Binary edge typing accepted: two types only, globally fixed. Structural edges define patterns, operational edges define transformations. Does not clutter rule space. Reduces isomorphism complexity via regime-separated matching.
Modular substrate approach adopted: start with base graphs as prototype, upgrade paths (hyperedges, weights) evaluated empirically against measured failures.
Substrate upgrades are manual researcher decisions at training start — not automated.
Two-level evaluation principle: every substrate decision evaluated for both the traveler (object level) and the second system (meta level).
Second system explicitly noted: represents traveler as graph, evolves it via graph rewriting, uses same substrate and interface. Design deliberately deferred.
Graph Operation Interface defined: five operation groups — structural query, transformation, compression evaluation, library interaction, substrate introspection.
open_questions_updated: q_06 expanded to hypergraphs and rule firing
q_09 layer hierarchy compensation for isomorphism cost
sessions.risk_assessment: Isomorphism cost is the primary technical risk. Second system stability is the primary design risk. Scope management is the primary execution risk. User's expectations calibrated — high risk, high reward, modular approach is correct mitigation.
sessions.memory_system_updates: session_loader upgraded to v0.6.3 with bold loading, online/offline model, context eviction. All index files updated with metadata (importance, tags, last_updated). Comparative memory solutions documented in notes.drafts d_01 with open-source optimization candidates.
sessions.project_setup: Project Perspective claude.ai project instructions drafted for future session entry point.
